person.name: Caravaggio
person.description: Michelangelo Merisi da Caravaggio[a] (also Michele Angelo Merigi or Amerighi da Caravaggio; 29 September 1571 – 18 July 1610), known mononymously as Caravaggio, was an Italian painter active in Rome for most of his artistic life. During the final four years of his life, he moved between Naples, Malta, and Sicily. His paintings have been characterized by art critics as combining a realistic observation of the human state, both physical and emotional, with a dramatic use of lighting, which had a formative influence on Baroque painting.

datapoints.description: Born and lived in Milan until age 5.

sources.title: Groq (Kimi K2) extraction from Wikipedia: Caravaggio

datapoints.description: Family fled plague; father and grandfather died here 1577. Grew up and trained locally.

sources.title: Groq (Kimi K2) extraction from Wikipedia: Caravaggio

datapoints.description: Arrived penniless; painted for Cesari, then del Monte; gained fame and Church commissions.

sources.title: Groq (Kimi K2) extraction from Wikipedia: Caravaggio

datapoints.description: Fled Rome after killing Tomassoni; re-established himself as leading painter.

sources.title: Groq (Kimi K2) extraction from Wikipedia: Caravaggio

datapoints.description: Sought papal pardon; joined Knights of St John; painted and was imprisoned here.

sources.title: Groq (Kimi K2) extraction from Wikipedia: Caravaggio

datapoints.description: Escaped Malta; painted in Syracuse, Messina, and elsewhere on the island.

sources.title: Groq (Kimi K2) extraction from Wikipedia: Caravaggio

datapoints.description: Returned; survived disfiguring assault while awaiting papal pardon.

sources.title: Groq (Kimi K2) extraction from Wikipedia: Caravaggio

datapoints.description: Died of fever (or possible murder/poisoning) while traveling from Naples to Rome.

sources.title: Groq (Kimi K2) extraction from Wikipedia: Caravaggio
